title: 9 Best Herbal Decoctions to Help with Fluid Retention Naturally

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for fluid retention because it helps to reduce inflammation in the body and improve the blood vessels' function, which allows the body to more efficiently remove excess fluids and reduce swelling.
Tannins are good for fluid retention because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help improve blood circulation and reduce water retention in the body.
Polyphenols are good for fluid retention because they help to increase urine production, which removes excess fluid from the body and reduces swelling.

Isorhapontigenin is good for fluid retention because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and improve blood circulation, allowing the body to naturally remove excess fluids and water that can cause fluid retention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a natural remedy that can help with fluid retention. The plant's leaves and roots contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. When used as a decoction, the water-soluble parts of the plant can be extracted and consumed as a liquid. The diuretic properties in Urtica dioica help increase urine production in the kidneys, which in turn helps the body to eliminate excess fluids and salts. This process can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the body, especially in the hands, feet, and face. Urtica dioica decoction also contains antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and improve overall fluid balance in the body. Additionally, the plant's diuretic properties can also help to lower blood pressure and improve circulation, further aiding in the reduction of fluid retention.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Angelica sinensis herbal decoctions that help with fluid retention, start by gathering 2-3 teaspoons of dried Angelica sinensis root. Place the root in a saucepan with 4 cups of water, and heat it over low-medium heat. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the flavor and potency of the decoction will be. Once the decoction has finished steeping, turn off the heat and let it cool slightly. Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. You can drink 1-2 cups of the decoction per day to help alleviate fluid retention. It's recommended to drink it warm, as this will help your body absorb the benefits of the Angelica sinensis root more effectively.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus decoctions have been traditionally used to help with fluid retention, also known as edema. This condition occurs when the body holds too much fluid, often in the hands, feet, and legs. The decoction is made from the root of the Astragalus plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds help to reduce swelling and relax the blood vessels, allowing the body to more easily get rid of excess fluid. Astragalus membranaceus also helps to improve kidney function, which is essential for removing waste and excess fluids from the body. When the kidneys are working properly, they can filter out the fluids that the body no longer needs, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus has been shown to have a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping the body to get rid of excess fluid more efficiently.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction that helps with fluid retention, you will need dried Angelica sinensis roots, a pot, and a strainer. First, rinse the dried roots under cold water, then put them in the pot and add 8-10 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 30-40 minutes. The longer it cooks, the stronger the decoction will be. Next, turn off the heat and let the decoction steep for 10-15 minutes. After the steeping time is up, use the strainer to strain the decoction into a separate container, discarding the roots. You can drink the decoction warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day. It's recommended to drink it on an empty stomach for better absorption. Also, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion root, is a natural remedy that may help with fluid retention. This is because the roots of the plant are rich in diuretic properties. Diuretics are substances that help the body remove excess water and salt. When we eat too much salt or drink too much fluid, our body holds onto the water, leading to swelling and discomfort. The diuretic properties in dandelion root help the kidneys to remove this excess water, reducing swelling and discomfort. Taraxacum officinale decoctions, made by boiling the roots in water, can be consumed as a tea or added to food to help the body get rid of excess fluid. Regular consumption may also help to lower blood pressure, as it contains potassium, a mineral that counteracts the effects of sodium in the body.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction that helps with fluid retention, start by gathering the roots of the Angelica sinensis plant, also known as Dong Quai. Clean and dry the roots thoroughly before using them. Use one to two teaspoons of the dried roots for every cup of water and place them in a saucepan. Pour in water, making sure the roots are fully covered, and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 20-30 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the decoction per day, ideally before meals, to help alleviate fluid retention and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus decoctions may help with fluid retention due to its diuretic properties. The plant contains compounds that increase urine production, allowing the body to release excess fluids and salts. This process helps reduce swelling and discomfort caused by water retention in the feet, ankles, and other body parts. The diuretic effects of Verbascum thapsus decoctions can also help lower blood pressure, as excess fluids in the bloodstream can contribute to high blood pressure. Furthermore, the decoction may have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with fluid retention. Regular use of Verbascum thapsus decoctions may also help regulate the body's fluid balance, preventing the buildup of excess fluids in the future.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction that helps with fluid retention, start by gathering 6-8 grams of dried Angelica sinensis root. Rinse the root in cold water to remove any impurities, and then chop it into small pieces. In a medium-sized pot, combine 2 cups of water and the chopped root. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool, then store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. To use, drink 1-2 cups of the decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals or before bedtime, as the herb is most effective when digested slowly. It's essential to note that Angelica sinensis can interact with certain medications and have side effects in large doses, so consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus, also known as artichoke, is a herb that has been used for centuries to help with fluid retention. The leaves and flowers of the plant are rich in compounds that can help reduce water retention in the body. When consumed as a decoction, these compounds are released and can help to improve kidney function. The kidneys play a key role in removing excess fluids from the body, and when they are functioning properly, fluid retention is reduced. Additionally, Cynara scolymus decoction contains diuretic properties that help the body to remove excess water and salt. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating and swelling in the feet and ankles. The diuretic properties also help to lower blood pressure, which is often a contributing factor to fluid retention. As the body's fluid levels decrease, the body's ability to remove waste products also improves, which can help to reduce the risk of conditions such as hypertension and cardiovascular disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction for fluid retention, start by gathering 6-8 grams of dried Angelica sinensis root. Rinse the root in clean water to remove any impurities. Next, boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan and add the Angelica sinensis root. Reduce the heat to a low simmer and let it steep for 10-15 minutes. After the steeping time, remove the saucepan from the heat and let it stand for 5 minutes to allow the root to settle at the bottom. Carefully pour the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, making sure to leave the root behind. Discard the root and let the decoction cool. Once cooled, you can store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours. To use, take 20-30 milliliters of the decoction, 2-3 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense is a type of plant that has been used for centuries to help with fluid retention. The decoction, or liquid extract, of this plant is made by boiling its roots and stems in water. The active ingredients in Equisetum arvense, such as flavonoids and saponins, are released during this process and are believed to have a diuretic effect. This means that they help the body to get rid of excess fluid, which can build up and cause swelling and discomfort. By increasing urine production, Equisetum arvense decoction can help to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, water retention, and swelling in the legs and feet. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Equisetum arvense may also help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with fluid retention.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis decoction for fluid retention, start by gathering dried Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes, which are available at most herbal stores. Measure out one to two teaspoons of the dried herbs for every cup of water you plan to use. In a medium-sized pot, combine the measured herbs with the water and bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds into the water. After the cooking time has passed, remove the pot from the heat and let the decoction steep for a few minutes before straining the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids and drink the decoction while still warm, ideally in the morning and afternoon to help alleviate fluid retention throughout the day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis decoctions can help with fluid retention because it reduces inflammation in the body. The decoctions from Althaea officinalis also have a gentle soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help prevent fluid from building up in the body. When the digestive system is healthy, it can function properly and help remove excess fluid from the body. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Althaea officinalis can help improve blood flow, which can aid in removing excess fluid from the body's tissues. Althaea officinalis decoctions can also help the kidneys function properly. When the kidneys are working well, they can efficiently remove excess fluid from the body. The decoctions contain compounds that support kidney health, which can help prevent fluid from building up in the body. By supporting kidney function, Althaea officinalis decoctions can help the body remove excess fluid naturally, reducing fluid retention and related symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction that helps with fluid retention, start by gathering dried Angelica sinensis roots or rhizomes. The dried roots should be crushed or chopped to release their medicinal properties. In a pot, combine 6-8 grams of the crushed Angelica sinensis with 1 liter of water. Heat the mixture over low heat, allowing it to simmer for 10-15 minutes. As the liquid reduces, its potency will increase. Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids. The resulting liquid should be a tea-like consistency. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the decoction per day, ideally in the morning and evening, to help alleviate fluid retention symptoms. Some people may experience better results by consuming the decoction with other herbs that also aid in digestion and fluid balance.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis decoction to help with fluid retention, start by gathering a handful of dried Angelica sinensis roots. Rinse them with clean water to remove any impurities. Next, chop the roots into smaller pieces to increase their surface area, allowing them to release their active compounds more efficiently. Place the chopped roots in a saucepan with about 2-3 cups of water, making sure the roots are fully submerged. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for about 20-30 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced slightly and the flavors have melded together. Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container, discarding the solids. Let the decoction cool, then drink 1-2 cups of it several times a day, ideally after meals, to help alleviate fluid retention and promote overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis decoctions have been used for centuries to help with fluid retention. The plant contains compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which help to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the body. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Angelica sinensis herbal decoction that helps with fluid retention, first, you need to gather dried Angelica sinensis roots. Next, add one to two teaspoons of the dried roots to a pot of boiling water, and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Let the mixture steep for about 10 to 15 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot, discarding the solids. You can drink the decoction warm or at room temperature, ideally 2 to 3 times a day, as needed. It's essential to note that Angelica sinensis decoctions are best consumed in moderation, and it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

intro_desc: Fluid retention, or edema, occurs when your body holds too much water, causing swelling in your feet, ankles, and hands. This can be caused by various factors, including hormonal changes, medication, and poor diet. Drinking herbal decoctions can help alleviate this issue. Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, is a natural diuretic that helps remove excess water from the body. Its leaves and roots contain compounds that stimulate the kidneys to produce more urine, reducing fluid buildup. Astragalus membranaceus, or astragalus, is another beneficial herb that can aid in fluid retention. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and improve circulation, allowing your body to more efficiently eliminate excess fluids.  Fixing fluid retention is essential because it can lead to serious health issues, such as heart problems and kidney damage, if left untreated. If you experience persistent swelling or discomfort, it's crucial to address the issue. Drinking herbal decoctions can be a natural and effective way to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms. By incorporating these herbs into your daily routine, you can improve your overall health and well-being. Drinking herbal decoctions can help you feel lighter and more energetic, allowing you to engage in daily activities with more ease. Additionally, reducing fluid retention can improve your mood and reduce fatigue, leading to a better quality of life.  To make herbal decoctions, you can steep the dried herbs in hot water for 5-10 minutes, then strain and drink. Start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed. It's also essential to maintain a balanced diet and lifestyle, which includes regular exercise, a healthy weight, and a well-rounded diet. By combining herbal decoctions with a healthy lifestyle, you can effectively manage fluid retention and improve your overall health.
supplementary_best: To remedy fluid retention, a combination of Equisetum arvense, Althaea officinalis, and Cynara scolymus is effective. Equisetum arvense, also known as horsetail, is a natural diuretic that helps remove excess water from the body. Althaea officinalis, or marshmallow root, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling caused by fluid retention. Cynara scolymus, or artichoke, has been shown to improve liver function and aid in the removal of toxins that can contribute to fluid retention. This combination works well because it addresses the root causes of fluid retention from multiple angles: diuretic action, inflammation reduction, and toxin removal.
supplementary_avoid: If you have fluid retention, there are some herbal decoctions you should avoid. For example, decoctions made from dandelion root (Taraxacum) are often used to help with water loss in the body, but if you already have fluid retention, this might make the problem worse.  Another herbal decoction to be cautious about is that of ginger (Zingiber officinale), as it can increase blood flow and cause the body to retain more fluid. Decoctions of burdock root (Arctium lappa) should also be used with care, as they can cause water retention in some people.  Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) decoctions are also not recommended for people with fluid retention. This is because licorice root can cause your body to hold onto water, making the problem worse.  You should also be careful with decoctions made from Eleutherococcus senticosus (Siberian ginseng), as they can cause an increase in blood pressure and fluid retention.
supplementary_other_remedies: Dietary changes can help alleviate fluid retention. Eating foods that are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, such as fatty fish and flaxseeds, can reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to fluid buildup. Additionally, foods high in magnesium like dark leafy greens and nuts can help regulate the balance of fluids in the body by promoting the elimination of excess fluids through urination.  Another natural remedy for fluid retention is regular exercise. Physical activity, especially aerobic exercises like walking and cycling, can help improve blood circulation, which can aid in the removal of excess fluids from the body. Furthermore, exercise can also help reduce stress levels, which is a known contributor to fluid retention.  Apple cider vinegar has also been used as a natural remedy for fluid retention. The acetic acid in apple cider vinegar helps to stimulate the digestion of food and promotes the elimination of excess fluids through urination. Drinking a mixture of apple cider vinegar and water before meals can help alleviate fluid retention.  Hot and cold compresses can also be used to help alleviate fluid retention. Applying a hot compress to the affected area can help increase blood flow and reduce swelling, while a cold compress can help constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid buildup.  The practice of dry brushing, which involves brushing the skin with a soft-bristled brush in circular motions, can also help reduce fluid retention. This practice can help improve circulation and promote the elimination of toxins and excess fluids through the skin.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with fluid retention often experience other health issues like high blood pressure, heart disease, and kidney problems. Medicinal herbs like hawthorn, dandelion root, and juniper berries can help. Hawthorn is a heart tonic that helps the heart pump blood more efficiently, reducing blood pressure. Dandelion root is a natural diuretic that increases urine production, helping the body get rid of excess water. Juniper berries also act as a diuretic, reducing fluid buildup and improving kidney function. Additionally, people with fluid retention may experience digestive issues like bloating, constipation, and stomach pain. Herbs like ginger and peppermint can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and improve digestion, while peppermint relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, easing cramps and bloating. Other herbs like turmeric and saw palmetto can help reduce inflammation and improve prostate health, which is often affected by fluid retention.
